title: Tom Brown’s School Days
description: Tom Brown’s School Days — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Tom Brown’s School Days" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Arthur
memes.description: The frail, devout, gentle-natured boy whose arrival transforms Tom Brown's moral life — the catalyst through whom the Doctor's strategy of character formation achieves its deepest victory

memes.label: Arthur's Illness
memes.description: The fever that nearly killed Arthur — the crisis that brought Tom to prayer and Dr. Arnold to a bedside vigil, marking the novel's emotional and spiritual climax

memes.label: Benjy
memes.description: The aged family servant of the Browns — Tom's childhood companion and protector, whose stories and kindness filled the boy's Berkshire years before Rugby claimed him

memes.label: Big-Side
memes.description: The senior level of Rugby School athletics — big-side football was the great house match played by the sixth-form and upper boys, and big-side hare-and-hounds was the cross-country paper chase that tested the mettle of every aspiring athlete

memes.label: Brooke's Speech
memes.description: Old Brooke's address to the Schoolhouse after the football match — the novel's moral manifesto, naming bullying as the house's central problem and pledging reform

memes.label: Brown Family Home
memes.description: The Berkshire home of Squire Brown — the warm, stable domestic world that formed Tom's character before Rugby claimed him

memes.label: Bullying at Rugby
memes.description: The endemic practice of older boys tormenting younger ones at Rugby School — the central moral problem that the novel's reform narrative addresses

memes.label: Calling-Over
memes.description: The daily roll-call ritual at Rugby School that bookended the school day and served as the primary mechanism of attendance and order

memes.label: The Calling-Over Ritual
memes.description: The daily assembly of the entire school for roll-call — a ritual that enacted the school's hierarchy and marked the daily transition from freedom to order

memes.label: Chapel at Rugby
memes.description: The spiritual centre of Rugby School life under Dr. Arnold — the scene of Sunday services, the Doctor's transformative sermons, and the burial place of the headmaster himself

memes.label: Charity Lamb
memes.description: Tom Brown's childhood nurse and early antagonist in his "war of independence."

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: The virtue of facing danger, pain, or social pressure without flinching — the quality that the novel presents as the foundation of all manly character

memes.label: Crab Jones
memes.description: Cool-headed Schoolhouse boy renowned as the best drop-kick "placer" in Rugby football, praised by Old Brooke as indispensable to the house team.

memes.label: Cricket at Rugby
memes.description: The game of cricket as played at Rugby School — a central recreational activity and character-forming sport in Tom Brown's school days.

memes.label: Dead Man's Lane
memes.description: The lane near Rugby School, mentioned in local lore, adding a note of darker history to the school's geography

memes.label: Diggs
memes.description: The eccentric older boy who advised and protected Tom and East during their persecution — a "very queer specimen of boyhood" whose rough exterior concealed a loyal heart

memes.label: Examinations
memes.description: The monthly formal assessments conducted personally by Dr. Arnold — dreaded occasions when the Doctor examined each form for one "long, awful hour" in the month's work, a cornerstone of his system of academic accountability

memes.label: Fagging
memes.description: The system of personal service by junior boys to senior boys at Rugby and other Victorian public schools

memes.label: The Fagging Rebellion
memes.description: Tom Brown and Harry East's organized resistance against the unlawful fagging imposed by Flashman — the pivotal event that transformed Tom from passive victim to moral actor

memes.label: Flashman
memes.description: The novel's central antagonist — a sixth-form bully whose corruption of the praepostor system drives the fagging rebellion and whose cowardice makes him the antithesis of the Rugby gentleman

memes.label: Flashman's Expulsion
memes.description: The disgrace and expulsion of Flashman after being caught drunk — the school's moral order reasserting itself against its most corrupt member, ending Part I with the triumph of justice over tyranny

memes.label: Flogging
memes.description: The system of corporal punishment by which masters maintained discipline at Rugby — a practice the novel treats as both necessary and morally troubling

memes.label: Football at Rugby
memes.description: The game of football as played at Rugby School — the sport's formative institution, featuring the legendary Schoolhouse match that opens Tom Brown's school career and embodies the muscular Christian ideal

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: The bonds of loyalty and affection between boys at Rugby — the novel's most intimate moral relationship, presented as the primary vehicle of character formation

memes.label: Friendship and Masculinity
memes.description: An analysis of the Tom-Arthur-East triangle as a model of Victorian male friendship — how the novel redefines masculinity through emotional bonds between boys and men

memes.label: The Gentleman Ideal
memes.description: The Victorian ideal of the Christian gentleman — the moral and social model that Rugby School aimed to produce through its fusion of classical education, athletic discipline, and Christian formation

memes.label: Gower
memes.description: East's chum at Schoolhouse; a practical-minded boy who resists Tom Brown's attempt to give up using cribs for second-lesson preparation.

memes.label: Green
memes.description: The leader of the fast set that Harry East fell in with while Tom was occupied with Arthur — representing the alternative social path that led away from moral seriousness

memes.label: Tom's First Hare-and-Hounds
memes.description: Tom Brown's first run at the school's paper-chase game — an initiation into the physical culture and camaraderie of Rugby's outdoor life

memes.label: Harry East
memes.description: Tom Brown's boon companion and fellow rebel — a good-hearted, spirited boy whose loyalty never wavered even when his moral development lagged behind his friend's

memes.label: Harry Winburn
memes.description: The best boy in Tom Brown's village — athlete, naturalist, and early mentor.

memes.label: Hedge
memes.description: Senior Schoolhouse football player who leads charges on Warner's wing and is named by Old Brooke among the half-dozen best players in the school.

memes.label: Holmes
memes.description: Sturdy, cheery praepostor of another house, trusted by the Doctor to administer corporal punishment for a bullying case, and one of "the best boys in the School."

memes.label: Honour at Rugby
memes.description: The code of personal honour that governed schoolboy conduct at Rugby — the moral currency by which character was measured and social standing determined

memes.label: Jack Raggles
memes.description: The long-stop on the Schoolhouse cricket eleven; the "toughest and burliest" of the team, known for his comic songs and feats of strength.

memes.label: Job Rudkin
memes.description: Son of widow Rudkin, a stolid village boy from the Vale of White Horse; Tom Brown's childhood companion and wrestling opponent.

memes.label: Johnson
memes.description: Schoolhouse cricketer; a fine bowler and steady batsman who distinguishes himself in the Marylebone match.

memes.label: The Keeper (Velveteens)
memes.description: Gamekeeper of the lands near Rugby School, known to the boys as "Velveteens"; catches Tom Brown poaching fish and brings him before the Doctor.

memes.label: Keeping-Up
memes.description: The rough initiation ritual at Rugby in which new boys were tossed in blankets — a tradition that blurred the line between welcome and hazing

memes.label: The Keeping-Up Incident
memes.description: Tom's first-night experience of the blanket-tossing ritual — interrupted by the praepostor's arrival, a mild version of the tradition that could easily become bullying

memes.label: Locking-Up
memes.description: The nightly ritual at Rugby School when all boys were required to be inside their houses, the gates were shut, and the school day formally ended — a fixed point around which the entire rhythm of boarding-school life revolved

memes.label: Lower-Fourth Form
memes.description: The largest and most notoriously disorderly form in Rugby's lower school — a critical stage in the academic hierarchy where Tom Brown fell into idleness and faced the temptations that tested his character

memes.label: Martin
memes.description: The old coach guard who delivered Tom Brown to Rugby — a warm-hearted servant whose kindness marked Tom's first encounter with the wider world beyond Berkshire

memes.label: Mary
memes.description: Schoolhouse servant who acted as go-between for the boys and the Doctor, and nursed Arthur through the fever.

memes.label: Mrs. Arnold
memes.description: Wife of Dr. Arnold, the headmaster of Rugby School; a background figure of domestic warmth who arranges Arthur's boarding with Tom Brown.

memes.label: Mrs. Brown
memes.description: Tom Brown's mother — a background presence whose love and anxiety for her son represent the domestic world that Rugby both supplanted and preserved

memes.label: Mrs. Wixie
memes.description: The "methodical, active, little dark-eyed woman" who serves as matron at Rugby Schoolhouse, stowing boys' linen and managing their domestic arrangements.

memes.label: Muscular Christianity
memes.description: The Victorian ideal uniting physical courage with Christian faith — the philosophy that shaped Rugby School under Dr. Arnold and that the novel champions through Tom Brown's transformation

memes.label: Old Brooke
memes.description: The ideal Rugby praepostor — captain of the Schoolhouse football team, moral leader of the house, and the model of manly Christian character whom Tom Brown aspired to emulate

memes.label: Old Thomas
memes.description: Verger and general servant at Rugby School; manages the distribution of journey-money at term-end and enforces the Doctor's orders about closing the Close at night.

memes.label: The Peacock Inn
memes.description: The coaching inn at Islington, London, where Tom Brown boards the Tally-ho coach at half-past two in the morning to begin his journey to Rugby School.

memes.label: Praepostor
memes.description: The sixth-form prefects who governed daily life at Rugby School, holding disciplinary and organizational authority over younger boys

memes.label: Rugby Chapel
memes.description: The school chapel at Rugby — the spiritual center of Dr. Arnold's educational project, where his legendary sermons were delivered from the oak pulpit

memes.label: Rugby Football
memes.description: The game of football as played at Rugby School — the central athletic institution of school life and the foundation of the Rugby code.

memes.label: Rugby School
memes.description: Rugby School as an institution — the great public school in Warwickshire that under Dr. Arnold's headmastership became the model of Victorian character education

memes.label: Sally Harrowell
memes.description: Keeper of the Schoolhouse tuck-shop, famed for her roast potatoes.

memes.label: The School Gates
memes.description: The main entrance gates of Rugby School — the threshold between the school world and the town, where boys arrive and depart, and where Tom pauses on his final return as a young man.

memes.label: Schoolhouse
memes.description: The headmaster's residence and boarding house at Rugby School — the social and architectural center of Tom Brown's school life, housing Dr. Arnold and the boys whose moral drama forms the novel's core

memes.label: Schoolhouse Eleven
memes.description: The Schoolhouse cricket team — the Big-side that competed against the rest of Rugby School in the summer game, embodying the house's sporting pride and competitive spirit

memes.label: The Schoolhouse Match
memes.description: The great annual football match pitting the Schoolhouse against the rest of the School — the novel's opening set piece and the event that established the values of courage, teamwork, and house loyalty

memes.label: Slogger Williams
memes.description: The bigger, stronger boy whom Tom Brown fought and defeated — a physical antagonist whose defeat demonstrated that righteous courage could overcome brute strength

memes.label: Sneaking
memes.description: The cardinal sin of the public school code — informing on a fellow boy to the masters, a violation of the honour code so severe it could result in complete social ostracism

memes.label: Squire Brown
memes.description: Tom Brown's father — a warm-hearted Berkshire Tory squire whose love, principles, and parting advice set the foundation for his son's character

memes.label: Sunday at Rugby
memes.description: The weekly rhythm of religious observance at Rugby School — compulsory chapel, Sunday lectures, and the peculiar atmosphere of the school's day of rest

memes.label: Tom's First Sunday Chapel
memes.description: Tom Brown's first experience of Sunday chapel at Rugby — a milestone in his moral education as he moved from restless boredom to genuine engagement with the service

memes.label: Tadpole
memes.description: One of the younger Schoolhouse boys — a fag and fellow-sufferer whose good-natured presence rounds out the portrait of the junior school community

memes.label: The Close
memes.description: The great school playing field at Rugby — the stage for football matches, fights, and the outdoor life of the school, dominated by its "noble elms"

memes.label: Dr. Arnold
memes.description: Dr. Thomas Arnold, the great headmaster of Rugby School — the moral architect whose philosophy of education through character transformed both the school and the novel's protagonist

memes.label: The Doctor's Victory
memes.description: Dr. Arnold's final conversation with Tom Brown — the culmination of eight years of patient moral cultivation, ending in the Doctor's complete victory over Tom's pride and self-will

memes.label: The Dormitory
memes.description: The shared sleeping quarters of the Schoolhouse — where boys talked after lights-out and the day's dramas were rehearsed in whispered conversations

memes.label: The Fight
memes.description: The ritualized combat between boys at Rugby — a quasi-judicial proceeding governed by strict rules of honour, conducted behind the chapel away from masters' eyes

memes.label: The Fighting Ground
memes.description: The secluded area behind Rugby Chapel where school fights were conducted — out of the masters' sight, governed by the boys' code of honour

memes.label: The Forms
memes.description: The hierarchical system of academic classes at Rugby — the formal structure that sorted boys by age and achievement and governed their daily lives

memes.label: The Great School
memes.description: The great schoolroom at Rugby — the vast hall where calling-over was conducted, lessons were recited, and the entire school assembled under the master's eye

memes.label: The Hall
memes.description: The great hall of the Schoolhouse — where boys ate, where Brooke delivered his speech, and where the community gathered for meals, singing, and the keeping-up ritual

memes.label: The Infirmary
memes.description: The school infirmary where Arthur was nursed through his fever — the setting for the novel's crisis of illness, prayer, and pastoral care

memes.label: The Kitchen
memes.description: The Schoolhouse kitchen where fags fetched meals and begged hot water for their praepostors — the domain of servants and the intersection of the boys' world with the adult world below stairs

memes.label: The Passage
memes.description: The corridors of the Schoolhouse — liminal spaces where boys gathered, gossiped, and negotiated the informal politics of house life

memes.label: The Quadrangle
memes.description: The central quadrangle of Rugby School — the paved courtyard at the heart of daily school life, linking the Schoolhouse, the hall, the studies, and the great gates.

memes.label: The Stage Coach
memes.description: The Tally-ho coach that carried Tom Brown from London to Rugby — a transitional space marking the journey from the old world of home to the new world of school

memes.label: The Studies
memes.description: The private rooms in the Schoolhouse assigned to senior boys — centers of power, friendship, and the informal moral education that the novel prized above formal instruction

memes.label: The Two Rugbys
memes.description: An analysis of the contrast between Rugby's official institutional life (chapel, praepostors, the Doctor's reforms) and its unofficial boy-governed world (fights, fagging abuse, pub culture) — and how this tension drove the novel's moral drama

memes.label: Thompson
memes.description: Schoolhouse boy whose death from fever during the outbreak marks a turning point in Tom's moral education.

memes.label: Tom Brown
memes.description: The protagonist of the novel — a spirited Berkshire boy who enters Rugby School and, over eight years, is formed into a Christian gentleman through friendship, trial, and the influence of Dr. Arnold

memes.label: Tom Brown's Moral Education
memes.description: A synthesis tracing Tom Brown's moral development across the novel — from untested country boy to morally awakened young man, shaped by friendship, suffering, and the influence of Dr. Arnold

memes.label: Tom's Arrival at Rugby
memes.description: Tom Brown's first arrival at Rugby School by coach — the moment of transition from the old world of Berkshire to the new world of public school, marking the beginning of his eight-year transformation

memes.label: Tom's Fight with Slogger Williams
memes.description: The climactic fistfight between Tom Brown and the physically superior Slogger Williams — Tom's victory demonstrated that righteous courage could overcome brute strength

memes.label: Tom Brown's Last Match
memes.description: Tom's final football match as a Schoolhouse boy — the culmination of his athletic career and a farewell to the physical life of the school

memes.label: Tossing
memes.description: The Rugby School initiation ritual in which new boys were tossed in a blanket by older boys — a test of pluck that separated the spirited from the timid, and the scene of Tom Brown's first public demonstration of courage

memes.label: Vale of White Horse
memes.description: The Berkshire countryside of Tom Brown's childhood — the pastoral world of Squire Brown's estate, village life, and the ancient White Horse that gave the region its name

memes.label: Walker
memes.description: A bigger Schoolhouse boy who, alongside Flashman, leads the tossing of new boys in Number 7; represents the casual cruelty of school initiation rituals.

memes.label: The War of Independence
memes.description: The organised resistance of the lower-school boys against the oppressive fagging system imposed by Flashman and the fifth-form bullies after the departure of the just sixth-form leaders — a pivotal episode in Tom Brown's moral development and the novel's argument about collective action against tyranny

memes.label: Warner
memes.description: A Schoolhouse praepostor who, alongside Brooke, helped maintain just rule — one of the "rough but strong and just" leaders whose departure marked the end of the house's golden age

memes.label: Willum (Willum Smith)
memes.description: Blacksmith's apprentice from the Vale of White Horse, defeated by the shepherd at the village backswording competition; later appears as Farmer Thompson's shepherd.
